key_metrics: Membership and other income: $1.65 billion (+5.4% YoY)
Other Gains: $2.71 billion (primary driver of net income growth)
qoq_changes: Total revenues increased to $177.40 billion in Q2 from an implied $165.61 billion in Q1.
Inventories increased to $57.73 billion from $56.44 billion at the end of the prior fiscal year (Jan 31, 2025).
new_risks: Core operating margin compression, evidenced by an 8.2% YoY decline in operating income despite 4.8% revenue growth.
Heavy reliance on non-operating gains to drive bottom-line growth this quarter (specific textual risk factors were omitted from the provided excerpt).
management_tone: Neutral; while top-line revenue and membership income continue to grow steadily, the contraction in operating income highlights underlying cost pressures that were masked by a one-time $2.7 billion non-operating gain.
analyst_note: Walmart delivered a complex quarter where a massive 56% YoY surge in net income was entirely driven by $2.7 billion in non-operating gains. Core retail operations showed margin compression, with operating income falling 8.2% YoY despite healthy 4.8% top-line revenue growth, warranting closer scrutiny on SG&A and cost of sales trends.
